comment: PROPOSED benchmark seeds (plan 4.5) — UNRATIFIED DRAFT awaiting user ratification at Gate C. Nothing here may be copied into eval/fixtures/ before ratification; once ratified, the seeds become eval/fixtures/seeds.json with a dated CHANGELOG.md entry and the file's SHA is pinned in the T1 amendment (PREREGISTRATION §9). This file contains seeds and constraints ONLY — no labels, no gate decisions, no telemetry. cmd/eval falls back to this draft with an UNRATIFIED warning until the ratified fixture exists. Validated by internal/eval/seeds_test.go.
procedure: Drafting procedure, stated before any eval run (PREREG §6 pins the target numbers; the fixture contents are versioned separately). Step 1 — dish family: pick 13 CANONICAL Western home-cook dishes (PREREG §6 locks v0 to the Western-cuisine subset). Canonical is deliberate: the dev prompt-iteration seeds are deliberately obscure so that no benchmark candidate could be tuned on (see the disjointness step).

Step 2 — safety-gate coverage: anchor at least one seed on every protein class the deterministic gate screens (data/safety/protein_classes.csv): poultry (bench-01 whole chicken, bench-02 ground turkey), ground_meat (bench-03 ground beef), whole_cut (bench-04 pork chop, bench-05 beef steak), fish (bench-06 salmon), shellfish (bench-07 shrimp, bench-08 clam), eggs (bench-09 egg). Every arm's proposals for these seeds must survive the same internal-temperature screening.

Step 3 — constraint coverage: one vegetarian case (bench-10), one vegan case (bench-11), and one allergen-constrained case (bench-12: 'tree nuts' against a classically pine-nut pesto, so the constraint does real work). Allergen values are FDA Big-9 wire values only.

Step 4 — variety: skill spans beginner/intermediate/advanced and servings span 2/4/6 across the set; equipment stays ordinary home-kitchen gear (the pinned Constraints shape carries it as free strings).

Step 5 — resolvability: every on_hand anchor resolves in the data/ingredients.csv universe via the grounding alias tables (asserted in internal/eval/seeds_test.go), so the grounded arm can actually resolve and cite every seed's anchor ingredients; seed texts name only dishes buildable from universe ingredients.

Step 6 — dev-seed disjointness (global rail: prompt iteration only ever ran against internal/llm/testdata/dev_seeds.json): the five dev dishes are 'savory rye porridge with mushroom broth and crispy shallots' (dev-01), 'celeriac schnitzel with caper brown butter' (dev-02), 'pan-roasted trout with sorrel butter sauce' (dev-03), 'juniper-spiced elk meatballs with braised red cabbage' (dev-04), and 'parsnip and pear soup with hazelnut gremolata' (dev-05). No benchmark seed shares an id, a dish, or an on_hand anchor with any of them; internal/eval/seeds_test.go asserts the disjointness.
expected_claims: Arithmetic (PREREG §6 target: ~200 labeled claims; spec §7 fixes the reading as ~200 TOTAL ACROSS ARMS, matching the double-label window): 13 seeds x 5 moves (eval/fixtures/move_script.json v1) x 3 arms (PREREG §4) x ~1 checkable claim per accepted move = 13 * 5 * 3 = 195 ~= 200 claims across arms. Double-label subset at PREREG §6's 15-20%: 0.15 * 195 = 29.25 and 0.20 * 195 = 39.0, so a 30-39-claim subset satisfies both the 15-20% band and the 30-40-claim window. Seed-count tuning: 12 seeds -> 180 and 15 -> 225, so 13 centers the estimate on the target; the ~1 claim/move rate is an estimate for the live model (roughly one new flavor_rationale[].claim or unverified[] entry per accepted creative move after per-dish dedup) — the deterministic stub emits fewer (~3 claims/seed), so stub dry-run counts read lower by design.

seeds.seed: herb-roasted whole chicken with root vegetables
baked turkey meatballs in tomato sauce
pan-seared pork chops with apples and sage
rosemary butter-basted steak with roasted potatoes
pan-seared salmon with lemon-dill yogurt sauce
garlic butter shrimp scampi with spaghetti
mushroom and spinach frittata with cheddar
butternut squash risotto with crispy sage
white bean and kale stew with tomatoes
basil pesto pasta with green beans and potatoes
roasted beet and goat cheese salad with walnuts
